seeding_instructions.important: REQUIRED STEP: Seed these actual meters with baseline history BEFORE sending test payloads!
seeding_instructions.seed_group_A: python utils/seed_normal_history.py --meter SE2303001 SE2303062 SE2303064 --group group_A --hours 48 --before '2026-06-18 10:00:00'
seeding_instructions.why_necessary: Without baseline history, rolling_mean = current reading, rolling_std = 0, z_score = 0. Statistical layer cannot fire. Test data must represent FUTURE readings of meters with seeded history.

group_A_tests.description: group_A: energy + voltage + current + power_factor | Real meters: SE2303001, SE2303062, SE2303064

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of SE2303001 at 2026-06-18 10:00:00, after 48h seeded history)

zscore_spike.description: Energy spike to 6.2Wh (+287% above baseline), z_score > 3.0, triggers z-score layer

zscore_drop.description: Energy near-zero (0.05Wh), sudden drop from baseline ~1.6Wh, triggers z-score layer

same_hour_deviation.description: Same timestamp hour (10:00) but energy 75% above historical avg for this hour, triggers same-hour deviation (>40%)

negative_energy.description: Negative energy (-1.2Wh), meter tamper indicator, triggers rule-based violation

power_factor_invalid.description: Power factor 1.05 (>1.0, physically impossible), triggers rule-based violation

power_factor_collapse.description: Power factor 0.65 (below 0.75 threshold), triggers rule-based violation

group_V_tests.description: group_V: voltage + current only (no energy) | Real meters: A0825001, A32600225

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of A0825001 after seeded history)

current_spike.description: Current spike to 5.0A (from ~1.4A baseline), z_score > 3.0, triggers z-score layer

group_E_tests.description: group_E: energy consumption only | Real meters: SE000021, SE000022

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of SE000021 after seeded history)

energy_spike.description: Energy spike to 5.2Wh (+325% above baseline), z_score > 3.0, triggers z-score layer

negative_energy.description: Negative energy (-0.8Wh), meter tamper indicator, triggers rule-based violation

group_B_tests.description: group_B: energy + apparent energy + voltage | Real meters: SE0000009, SE0000011

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of SE0000009 after seeded history)

energy_anomaly.description: Energy spike to 4.8Wh with voltage sag to 175V, triggers both rule-based (sag) and z-score layers

group_C_tests.description: group_C: energy + current only | Real meters: SE1338167, SE2104744

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of SE1338167 after seeded history)

current_spike.description: Current spike to 4.8A with energy increase, z_score > 3.0, triggers z-score layer

group_D_tests.description: group_D: full feature set including frequency | Real meters: M1GEMAC1, M2GDDAC1

normal.description: Normal baseline reading (future reading of M1GEMAC1 after seeded history)

frequency_anomaly.description: Frequency drops to 48.5Hz (outside 49-51Hz safe range), triggers rule-based violation

batch_request_example.description: Example batch POST /detect request with multiple anomalies from different meters

critical_workflow: 1. SEED BASELINE: Run ALL seeding commands at the top of this file for each capability group. Use --before '2026-06-18 10:00:00' so history ends when test data begins.
2. WAIT: Give database ~2-3 seconds to commit seeded history before sending test payloads.
3. SEND TEST: Use /detect endpoint with records from the test sections below. Each record is a FUTURE reading of a seeded meter.
4. OBSERVE LOGS: Check terminal running uvicorn for per-stage timing and trigger reasons.
Without seeded history, rolling_mean = current reading, rolling_std = 0, z_score = 0. Statistical layer cannot fire!

layer_triggers.rule_based: Voltage sag (<180V) or swell (>270V), negative energy, invalid power factor (>1.0 or <0.75), frequency out of range (49-51Hz)
layer_triggers.zscore: Reading is 3+ standard deviations from rolling baseline (uses historical window only), OR same-hour deviation > 40%
layer_triggers.isolation_forest: Complex multivariate anomalies detected by trained ML model (requires model artifacts in models/ directory)

curl_examples: Spike (should fire z-score): Use SE2303062 zscore_spike payload from group_A_tests
Voltage sag (should fire rule-based): Use SE2303062 voltage_sag payload from group_A_tests
Check logs: tail -f or monitor terminal running 'uvicorn api.main:app --reload'

expected_behavior.normal_readings: All three layers run, no anomalies detected. Baseline rolls forward.
expected_behavior.z_score_spike: Rule-based: OK. Z-score: TRIGGERED (reading 3+ σ from baseline). Isolation Forest: may trigger. Anomaly persisted with flagged_anomalous=TRUE.
expected_behavior.voltage_sag: Rule-based: TRIGGERED (VOLTAGE_SAG). Z-score: depends on magnitude. Isolation Forest: may trigger. Anomaly persisted.
expected_behavior.same_hour_deviation: Rule-based: OK. Z-score: TRIGGERED (SAME_HOUR_DEVIATION_EXCEEDED). Baseline stays clean (flag=FALSE for normal sameday).
